category_sources: Official TinTin++ website describes TinTin++ as a MUD client with triggers, aliases, macros, mapping, sessions, and scripting.
source_facts.description says it is a MUD client.
display_name_sources: Official GitHub repository description says TinTin++, aka tt++, is an extensible console MUD client.
source_facts.homepage is the official TinTin++ website.
docs_sources: Official TinTin++ manual is hosted on mudhalla.net and the official site links TinTin++ FAQ material.
repo_sources: source_facts.repo already provided; per instruction, repo returned as null and not second-guessed.
tags_sources: Official TinTin++ site lists client features including scripting, sessions, triggers, aliases, macros, and mapper support.
